title: Watch: Bat blocked by Dickwella's foot? Priyansh Arya's freak run-out triggers debate
content: A strange mix-up saw Priyansh Arya run out in the tri-series opener against Sri Lanka A.  His bat collided with the wicketkeeper's foot while trying to make his ground.  Ruturaj Gaikwad scored a century to rescue India A after an early collapse.  Late contributions helped India A post a competitive total.

title: India deploys 12 nuclear warheads for first time; where do China and Russia stand?
content: India has deployed 12 nuclear warheads for the first time, a significant shift in its nuclear posture, according to SIPRI. With a total of 190 warheads, this move signals a move towards a more operationally ready sea-based deterrent. This development occurs as global nuclear arsenals expand, with China rapidly increasing its stockpile.

title: Protests erupt outside Apple headquarters, demand crackdown on Elon Musk's Grok
content: Protesters targeted Apple's WWDC 2026, demanding the tech giant clean up its App Store. Advocacy groups highlighted 47 'nudify' apps, which use AI to create nonconsensual sexualized images, and Apple's alleged earnings from them, including xAI's Grok. Concerns were also raised about child sexual abuse material in iCloud, with calls for renewed action.

title: NEET UG 2026 re-test: Pradhan reviews NTA readiness, tightens security and coordination
content: NEET UG 2026: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan reviewed NEET-UG 2026 re-test preparations at NTA headquarters in Delhi ahead of June 21. As per ANI, he stressed strict confidentiality across all stages, from question paper setting to distribution. He highlighted coordination with departments, states and central agencies, including a Cabinet Secretary-led meeting and a planned review by the Union Home Secretary. He also noted new administrative appointments and cyber security measures involving IB and CBI support for examination integrity.

title: Bezos wants people to stop blaming Airbnb for high rents in New York city; here's why
content: Jeff Bezos argued on CNBC that government policies limiting housing supply, not Airbnb, are the primary drivers of high rents in New York City. He cited zoning and permitting restrictions as key factors, stating that subsidizing demand while constraining supply inevitably leads to soaring prices. Bezos also criticized corporate welfare and special tax provisions as forms of crony capitalism.

title: Poaching fears? Congress relocates Madhya Pradesh MLAs to Karnataka, ahead of RS polls
content: Amidst Rajya Sabha polls in Madhya Pradesh, Congress MLAs are being moved to Karnataka, with party leaders alleging BJP poaching attempts. Congress claims BJP offered bribes to its legislators. The move aims to prevent cross-voting and secure their candidate's victory, recalling the 2020 government collapse.

title: Setback for Trump as US judge strikes down $100,000 fee: How it impacts H-1B visa aspirants
content: A US federal judge has blocked President Trump's controversial $100,000 fee for new H-1B visas. The judge ruled the administration overstepped its authority by imposing the charge, calling it an unauthorized tax. This decision offers relief to many aspiring foreign workers, particularly from India, and companies relying on skilled talent.

title: ‘Not Nvidia’: Cerebras CEO reveals strategy to break AI giant’s grip
content: Cerebras Systems is actively forging partnerships across the AI infrastructure landscape, notably excluding Nvidia. The company is integrating its wafer-scale processors with offerings from various hardware manufacturers and cloud providers, aiming to be a flexible component in diverse AI systems. Recent agreements with Amazon and OpenAI highlight Cerebras' strategy to supply hardware for AI workloads beyond a single ecosystem.

title: DU professor's murder: Recces, WhatsApp calls and forged IDs point to a plot mapped in advance
content: A Delhi University professor's murder on June 3 appears to have been premeditated by her tenants, who were reportedly angered by her refusal to sell her ancestral home. The couple allegedly conducted reconnaissance and planned the murder after the professor agreed to meet them on June 3.

title: DK Shivakumar takes bites from apples, throws them into crowd; video goes viral
content: Karnataka chief minister DK Shivakumar's public gesture of biting apples from a ceremonial garland and tossing them to supporters has sparked a viral debate. While some hail it as an affectionate interaction, others criticize the act of distributing partially eaten food. The incident, captured in Kanakapura, has drawn mixed reactions online, prompting discussions about its appropriateness.

title: TDS on property purchase above Rs 50 lakh explained: Rules, conditions and filing process
content: Property buyers purchasing assets valued at Rs 50 lakh or more must deduct 1% TDS before completing the transaction. This rule, under Section 194-IA, applies to non-agricultural land and aims to boost tax compliance in real estate. Buyers must deposit the tax and issue a certificate to the seller, ensuring accurate reporting of high-value deals.

title: Siraj ruled out of Ireland and England T20Is; Krishna named replacement
content: India pacer Mohammed Siraj has been withdrawn from the T20I squads for the Ireland and England tours as part of a workload management programme. The BCCI said the move was precautionary to ensure adequate recovery before a busy season. Prasidh Krishna has been named as his replacement in the Shreyas Iyer-led squad, which also includes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

title: Zojila tunnel breakthrough: Road Pakistan wanted to choke during Kargil gets all-weather shield
content: A major milestone has been achieved in the Zojila Tunnel project, connecting its two ends to create a 13-km passage. This vital infrastructure will ensure all-weather connectivity between Kashmir and Ladakh, eliminating annual disruptions and significantly reducing travel time. Expected to be operational by 2028, the tunnel is crucial for military logistics and regional development.

title: Strait of Hormuz closure: Why high oil prices may be a temporary shock only - explained
content: “Oil prices will be lower if Hormuz reopens earlier. Uncertainty remains high regarding the timing of Hormuz reopening, and oil prices will remain volatile as a result,” Fitch says.

title: Court orders AirAsia to pay farmer Rs 90,750 after delayed flight damaged rare jackfruit plant
content: A farmer's hybrid jackfruit sapling was ruined after AirAsia's flight delay caused him to miss his connection. The consumer commission ordered AirAsia to pay Rs 90,750 for the damaged sapling, refunding ticket fare, reimbursing expenses, and compensating for mental agony and deficiency in service. The airline failed to appear, leading to an ex-parte ruling.

title: Jagapathi Babu thanks 'Peddi' critics for negative reviews of the film
content: Jagapathi Babu lauded Ram Charan's pivotal role in 'Peddi', calling him 'Superman' for shouldering the film. He also humorously thanked critics for negative reviews, stating they inadvertently boosted the movie's earnings. The film's success was celebrated, with Babu highlighting the team's resilience in overcoming challenges. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Watch: Arjun Tendulkar turns fan's pain into cherished memory with lovely gesture
content: Arjun Tendulkar won hearts in the Mumbai T20 League after checking on a young fan accidentally hit on the head by one of his sixes. He later gifted the child a signed match ball. Arjun also starred on the field, taking 3/11 and scoring an unbeaten 66 as Arcs Andheri cruised to a nine-wicket win over Bandra Blasters.

title: 'Could've been murdered by contract killers if ...': Ex-TMC MP's big claim after quitting party
content: Days after leaving the Trinamool Congress, Sukhendu Sekhar Ray has leveled severe accusations against Mamata Banerjee's party, labeling it a "party of thieves, rapists." He revealed a chilling fear of being "murdered by contract killers" had he departed during the RG Kar hospital controversy, an incident that solidified his decision to leave.

title: 'Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai' box office collection: Varun Dhawan film eyes Rs 30cr milestone
content: David Dhawan's 'Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai' is carrying its opening weekend momentum steadily into the weekdays. After a rock-solid Rs. 24 crore net opening weekend and a healthy Monday hold of Rs. 3.50 crore on Day 4, the Varun Dhawan, Mrunal Thakur and Pooja Hegde starrer's domestic net stands at Rs. 27.80 crore with Day 5 to hold on to the momentum. With a worldwide gross already at Rs. 42.43 crore and evening shows yet to be tallied, a first-week domestic net in the Rs 35–40 crore range remains firmly within reach for the season's dominant family entertainer.

title: Did Trump ask Netanyahu not strike Iran? US President clarifies his position
content: US President Donald Trump stated he urged Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to end hostilities with Iran quickly, not to refrain from retaliation. Trump expressed confidence a nuclear deal with Tehran could be reached within days, suggesting both nations agreed to halt recent exchanges of attacks.

title: Fitch trims India's growth forecast to 6.4% as Middle East turmoil clouds outlook
content: Fitch Ratings has lowered India's FY27 GDP forecast to 6.4% due to rising costs from Middle East conflict impacting consumer spending. While domestic demand remains a driver, higher prices are expected to reduce purchasing power, with the most visible impact in Q2 and Q3 of FY27.

title: Himanshu Jangra’s ₹370 biryani remark sparks debate on dating and consent
content: A ₹370 biryani comment made by Himanshu Jangra during a comedy show sparked a nationwide debate on dating, consent, and transactional relationships. The incident, amplified by social media, led to widespread criticism of the mindset behind the joke and prompted a comedian's apology, highlighting societal attitudes towards modern dating.

title: TMC vs TMC intensifies: Mamata camp dares 'traitors' to resign and fight on BJP ticket
content: Trinamool MP Kalyan Banerjee fiercely criticized rebel leader Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, labeling her faction "desperate" and accusing them of betraying Mamata Banerjee and colluding with the BJP. Dastidar, however, remained defiant, vowing to fight for Bengal and the nation, asserting her long-standing commitment to the party and its principles. This exchange highlights a deepening internal conflict within the TMC.

title: Watch: Arjun Tendulkar shines with bat and ball, gets big dressing-room applause
content: Arjun Tendulkar starred for Arcs Andheri in the T20 Mumbai League 2026, delivering a match-winning all-round performance. He took 3/11 with the ball and then smashed an unbeaten 66 to guide his team to a nine-wicket victory. Captain Shivam Dube lauded Tendulkar's effort, highlighting his crucial role in the win.

title: ITR filing FY 2025-26: Top things salaried taxpayers should know
content: Apart from Form 16, other important documents that you should keep handy are Form 26AS, Annual Information Statement (AIS), bank account interest earnings, any investment related documents etc.

title: India may have lost a key sixth-gen fighter option as China races ahead with flying prototypes
content: India's pursuit of sixth-generation fighter jets faces a setback as a key European program, the Franco-German-Spanish FCAS, encounters uncertainty. New Delhi was eyeing collaboration with either FCAS or the UK-Japan-Italy GCAP. These advanced aircraft promise revolutionary capabilities, including AI integration and manned-unmanned teaming, as global powers race ahead.

title: CBSE marking row pushes Bengaluru students towards Karnataka PU colleges
content: Concerns over evaluation errors in CBSE’s on-screen marking (OSM) system are prompting Class 11 students in Bengaluru to shift from CBSE schools to PU colleges. Parents and students fear lower scores in core subjects such as Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics could affect professional course admissions, where board marks are considered alongside CET scores. School administrators report multiple withdrawals despite fee losses, while educators say the controversy is hurting CBSE admissions and may have a greater impact next year.

title: ED summons Pinarayi Vijayan's daughter Veena in money laundering case
content: T. Veena, daughter of former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan, has been summoned by the Enforcement Directorate for questioning in a money laundering case. The probe centers on alleged financial transactions between Cochin Minerals and Rutile Ltd (CMRL) and her company, Exalogic Solutions. ED claims CMRL made payments of Rs 2.78 crore for IT consultancy, generating proceeds of crime.

title: Karnataka's 5.2 floor area ratio limit: Check how it could impact homebuyers
content: Karnataka has significantly boosted its Floor Area Ratio (FAR) for industrial zones, allowing developers to build taller structures. This move, particularly impactful for Bengaluru's booming real estate market, aims to increase housing supply and potentially ease soaring property prices. However, developers will incur premium fees, which might influence buyer costs.

title: SpiceJet's cash crunch deepens: Many pilots unpaid since March, airline seeks emergency loan
content: SpiceJet pilots and employees are still awaiting salaries owed since March, as the airline seeks an emergency government-backed loan. Internal messages reveal significant financial strain, with employees struggling to manage daily expenses. The airline acknowledges payment delays, attributing them to operational challenges and the Middle East crisis.

title: Ben Stokes controversy: What happened at the Chelsea nightclub?
content: Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson are under ECB investigation after breaching a midnight curfew and becoming involved in a nightclub altercation in Chelsea. Reports claim a Saracens academy rugby player threw a punch at Atkinson but hit an ECB security guard, who needed stitches. ECB sources insist the cricketers “were not the aggressors.” Stokes’s captaincy is now under scrutiny.

title: Rs 1.2 lakh penalty on postal department for losing customer’s Pashmina shawls parcel
content: The Baramulla Consumer Commission has delivered a decision, holding postal services accountable for the mishap involving a lost parcel that contained three luxurious Pashmina shawls, collectively valued at Rs 60,000. Despite the postal authorities' reliance on the Indian Post Office Act, the Commission ruled against them, identifying a concrete deficiency in the service provided.

title: A menacing message: Why Iran's latest strikes against Israel are different
content: Iran's recent missile and drone strikes on Israel, though militarily minor, signal a significant strategic shift. Tehran now views attacks on its proxy as direct assaults, expanding its red lines and increasing regional risks. This move aims to bolster its credibility with allies and leverage in diplomatic talks, potentially normalizing limited force as a diplomatic tool.

title: What is circle rate and how does it affect how much you pay for a new home?
content: Understanding a property's 'circle rate' is crucial for buyers in India. This government-set minimum value, also known as reckoner or guidance value, dictates stamp duty and registration charges, often exceeding the actual sale price. Location, property type, and infrastructure significantly influence these rates, impacting your final cost and negotiation power. Always verify the circle rate before purchasing.

title: More measures on gold coming? Finance ministry asks banks for information on gold loans
content: Despite a lower import volume of 721 tonnes compared with the previous year, India's gold import bill rose 24% to a record $71.9 billion in 2025-26.

title: How many runs did Sooryavanshi score vs SL A after stellar IPL season?
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s first outing for India A after his impressive IPL 2026 season ended with a 14-run knock off 12 balls against Sri Lanka A in Dambulla. The teenager hit three fours before falling to Mohamed Shiraz. Ahead of the match, Sri Lanka A vice-captain Niroshan Dickwella had praised Sooryavanshi’s talent and said his team had plans to keep him in check.

title: 'Batwara 1947' motion poster: Sunny Deol drops first glimpse; announces release date officially
content: Get ready for an emotional ride as 'Batwara 1947' featuring Sunny Deol and directed by Rajkumar Santoshi reveals its first motion poster! Set against the heart-wrenching backdrop of India’s partition, the film stars a stellar cast including Preity Zinta, Shabana Azmi, and Karan Deol. Originally titled 'Lahore 1947', audiences can anticipate its theatrical debut on August 14, 2026. Read on to know more in detail.

title: More than Butter Chicken: Why India can't stop craving Punjabi food
content: Punjabi cuisine, a cornerstone of Indian comfort food, has endured global culinary shifts by embodying emotion, hospitality, and home. From ancient roots to post-Partition dhabas and modern restaurants, its rich flavors and hearty simplicity continue to resonate, offering a taste of tradition and belonging that nourishes the soul.

title: 'Gukesh can take inspiration from Pragg': Anand advises world champion
content: Viswanathan Anand praised R Praggnanandhaa’s Norway Chess triumph, calling his comeback and fighting style impressive. He urged world champion D Gukesh, who struggled in the event, to take inspiration from Praggnanandhaa’s persistence and hard work. Anand also highlighted the healthy rivalry among India’s top young stars and said India is now firmly among the world’s leading chess nations.

title: 96 hours in no-man's land: BSF shelters Bangladeshi family pushed back by BGB
content: India intervened to shelter a Bangladeshi family of ten stranded near the Jalpaiguri border after Bangladesh refused their return, allegedly seizing their documents. Initially allowed into Bangladesh, they were pushed back within a day. Videos suggest BGB personnel instigated villagers to seize documents, then claimed the family was Indian.

title: Torso of unidentified man found in suitcase at Chennai’s Perambur railway station
content: Police are verifying missing persons complaints and coordinating with police stations across Chennai and neighbouring districts to identify the deceased

title: Ministry, NTA, NMC officials to face parliamentary panel on health tomorrow over NEET re-exam
content: This is the third parliamentary panel before whom the top officials of the Ministries and NTA (National Testing Agency) and NMC (National Medical Commission) are appearing

title: Police file chargesheet in Aluva Athul murder case in Kerala
content: According to police, the murder was the fallout of a long-standing enmity between Athul’s group and an organised criminal gang operating out of Thazhava Kadathoor region in Kollam

title: T.N. govt. must ensure senior citizens are provided safe shelter, food, and medical care: Tamilisai Soundararajan
content: She urged CM Vijay to urgently identify elderly persons living without adequate family or social support, in light of an incident in Namakkal where three senior citizens ended their lives

title: Schoolchildren participate in TVK function in Salem district; Education department launches inquiry
content: A function was held to open the local TVK office at Thippampatti in the Veerapandi Assembly constituency in Salem. Around 50 students from Kuralnatham Government Primary School were allegedly brought by the headmaster in school uniforms to attend the event

title: Gujarat identifies 5.96 lakh ‘Lakhpati Didis’ under rural livelihood mission
content: The State has identified 10 lakh potential beneficiaries under the Lakhpati Didi initiative, a programme aimed at helping women associated with SHGs achieve annual household incomes exceeding ₹1 lakh through entrepreneurship and livelihood activities

title: Haryana sets December 2027 deadline for Yamuna pollution-control projects; Chief Secretary reviews progress
content: In a major step towards rejuvenating the Yamuna river, the Haryana government has launched an extensive action plan, focussing on sewage treatment, industrial waste management and real-time monitoring of drain pollution

title: Anbumani meets T.N. CM Vijay, urges caste-based survey announcement in Governor’s address
content: Mr. Anbumani told journalists that he met the Chief Minister with the resolution passed at a multi-party meeting chaired by him last week, in which representatives of 42 political parties and organisations participated and “unanimously” sought a caste-based survey in the State

title: West Bengal CID reaches Mamata Banerjee’s residence in forgery allegations
content: After a standoff with party supporters, a team of half a dozen CID officials entered the residence of the former CM

title: Security forces recover huge cache of arms, ammunition from Maoist dump in Odisha's Malkangiri
content: It is suspected that these explosives, along with other materials, were intended for Maoist cadres and were intended to use them against innocent civilians and security forces

title: Two held for posing as Kala Jathedi gang members to issue extortion threat to Delhi businessman
content: Businessman Rohit Jain approached Adarsh Nagar police station with a complaint that he had been receiving threatening calls from international numbers

title: ‘Not just about giving money’: Lokesh pledges long-term support for steel blast families
content: The IT Minister says the families will get the first ₹25 lakh at once and talks in Amaravati later, and rejects Opposition charges linking the accident to VSP’s privatisation

title: DSS to hold rallies on June 16 seeking Karnataka Cabinet berth for H.C. Mahadevappa
content: Karnataka State Dalit Sangharsha Samiti has announced a Statewide rally on June 16, demanding a Cabinet berth for senior Congress leader and former Minister H.C. Mahadevappa in the proposed expansion of the State Cabinet

title: MSC Elsa 3 sinking: Kerala HC permits three crew members to return to home countries
content: HC grants permission to travel back since they are arrayed as accused in the case registered before Judicial First Class Magistrate, Kochi, and their presence was only needed, if at all, as witnesses in proceedings before the Magistrate or High Court

title: Vacant defence land in Uttar Pradesh to host 250 MW solar power plant
content: The Defence Ministry said the initiative reflects the government's commitment towards clean energy, sustainability and reduction of dependence on conventional energy sources

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker drops action against 21 AIADMK MLAs after Palaniswami condones them
content: J.C.D. Prabhakar said proceedings had been initiated against four rebel legislators who resigned and joined the TVK

title: Make alternative arrangements for Cauvery delta farmers, Dhinakaran urges TVK regime
content: His comments come in the wake of the uncertainty of the Mettur dam being opened on the scheduled date of June 12

title: Manapparai murukku makers face price rise crunch
content: The war in West Asia has triggered a steep rise in the price of various inputs and the packaging material, squeezing the margins for the makers of the famous Manapparai murukku

title: Congress’ electoral strategy weakened TMC, CPI(M) and DMK: Thirumavalavan
content: Speaking at a meeting of the INDIA bloc in New Delhi, he says the party’s approach has caused dissatisfaction among several constituents and calls upon it to explore ways to further strengthen and consolidate the alliance

title: House pattas will be given to eligible SC/ST families within 5 years: Minister Vanni Arasu
content: He says the initiative to grant house e-pattas was among the government’s top priorities and improving the condition of SC/ST hostels is another of its priorities

title: Former TMC MLA and first mayor of Bidhnanagar held by Kolkata police
content: Sabyasachi Dutta has been a two-time MLA from Trinamool Congress who also switched to BJP in 2019 but returned to his old party two years later

title: ‘Next-Billion AI Index’ to redefine artificial intelligence adoption in India
content: It argues that the metrics dominating AI development in the world are essentially useless for the billion-plus users across India and the Global South

title: Irregular collection of garbage leads to proliferation of blackspots in Bengaluru
content: The NammaKasa portal, which tracks blackspots in the city, reported 5,445 active blackspots on Sunday (June 7); just a month earlier, the number stood at 3,200

title: Parliamentary panel seeks views on bill to tweak corporate laws
content: Introduced in March this year, the legislation proposes comprehensive amendments to both the Companies Act, 2013, and the Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) Act, 2008

title: Landmark feat: ‘Breakthrough’ in Zojila tunnel achieved
content: The breakthrough has brought the decades-old dream of all-weather connectivity between the Kashmir Valley and Ladakh closer to reality

title: Pawan Kalyan questions VSP safety after blast, flags similar incident the night before
content: Deputy CM stresses need for upgradation of VSP and also expresses displeasure over delay in allotment of captive mines; Decides to hold a meet with workers union soon

title: Kerala govt seeks Centre’s permission to use forest land for Wayanad MCH expansion
content: This is in return for a similar parcel of land. Health Minister open State-level launch of ‘Kayakalpam’ initiative to gather public opinion on deficiencies in health system and patient care services

title: Measures under way to simplify process of obtaining community certificates: T.N. Minister Sengottaiyan
content: A survey would be conducted to identify lands belonging to the State government across Tamil Nadu and study how they could be put to productive use, he said

title: NEET-UG 2026 retest: IAF to transport question papers for June 21 medical entrance examination
content: The NEET-UG, 2026 retest question papers will be transferred by the IAF to 18 locations across the country, which will later be transported securely to the different examination centres

title: National Testing Agency announces NEET-UG re-exam on June 21
content: On May 12, the NTA had announced the cancellation of NEET-UG and said the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) would carry out a comprehensive inquiry into the alleged irregularities linked to the exam

title: Plea at Madurai Bench of Madras High Court to enhance relief assistance to fishermen during annual fishing ban
content: Unless adequate compensation was granted, the fishermen families would be pushed into debt traps, bonded labour, exploitative private loans and forced migration during every fishing ban season, the petitioner said.

title: SAIL, NMDC explore Russian coking coal assets, nickel supplies
content: Both SAIL ‌and ⁠NMDC are exploring sourcing of raw materials and are in ⁠talks with Russia, and SAIL has also set up an internal panel to study the issue

title: Former Maharashtra Minister files plea in Supreme Court against CBSE's three-language rule for Class 9 students
content: The plea filed by Fauzia Khan, an educationist and former Maharashtra Minister, contends that the CBSE's May 15 circular is arbitrary and unreasonable

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay reviews MAWS schemes, stresses timely completion of projects
content: The Chief Minister also called for restoration of the Cooum river and Buckingham canal along with the ongoing Adyar River Restoration project

title: Indian envoy meets U.S. tech leaders; discusses perspectives on future of AI
content: In June last year, the then leadership of Walmart Inc met Prime Minister Narendra Modi and discussed the U.S.-based retail giant’s growing partnership with India

title: NEET-UG, 2026 June 21 retest: Bihar Police caution aspirants, parents against rumours, fraud calls
content: The police advisory said ‘those found guilty of engaging in such activities will face severe consequences, including legal actions’

title: Power cuts leave Chennai residents fuming; protests erupt across city
content: At around 2.30 a.m., nearly 100 residents laid siege to the EB office in Arumbakkam, demanding immediate restoration of power; similar protests were witnessed in Washermenpet, Tiruvottiyur, Ambattur, Madhavaram, Manali, Avadi, Poonamallee, Thirunindravur, Medavakkam, and Velachery

title: Heavy rains, strong winds batter parts of Kerala
content: Authorities have advised people living in vulnerable areas to remain vigilant in view of the continuing monsoon activity

title: ED raids in Punjab: BJP misusing Central agencies to harass traders, says CM Bhagwant Mann
content: Attacking the BJP, whom the AAP often calls "ED party", Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann said, "The ED party wants to harass ourtraders by misusing Central agencies.”

title: Muslim Community representatives, civil societies to establish task force to tackle hate crimes
content: “The meeting is not a confrontation against any government or political party; rather, it is a sincere effort to find sustainable and practical solutions to the issues faced by Muslims,” said Maulana Ilyas Khan Falahi, president, Jamaat-e-Islami Maharashtra

title: Modi government intoxicated on power: Congress on reduction in number of subsidised Ujjwala refills
content: The Congress president said the LPG prices have been hiked repeatedly and 5.56 crore beneficiaries are unable to afford refills

title: Supreme Court flags 40-year delay by Allahabad HC in murder case, seeks solutions to judicial backlog
content: In his appeal before the Supreme Court, Vijay Singh highlighted the prolonged delay, stating that he is now 72 years old and has spent his youth, middle age and old age under the shadow of a criminal conviction

title: Latur tribunal cancels gift deed after man fails to take care of 89-year-old grandmother
content: The obligation to care for senior citizens is not merely a moral responsibility, but a legally enforceable condition when property is transferred on such assurances

title: 24 Indian sailors rescued from ship hit by U.S. missile
content: Marivex sanctioned by the U.S. last December, had earlier carried Iranian oil to India; U.S. Central Command says the tanker violated the ongoing blockade by attempting to sail to an Iranian port; sailors likely to reach India in two days

title: Lottery baron Santiago Martin, MLA wife Leema Rose and daughter Daisy move Madras High Court against ED action
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan order notice to the Central agency on batch of 39 appeals filed against the attachment of properties worth ₹910.29 crore

title: 28,000 vehicles an hour and growing; Cyberabad bets on  flyovers and underpasses to ease Allwyn-Miyapur traffic snarls
content: The road project includes a 1.79-km bi-directional bridge and flyover covering both the Miyapur and Allwyn junctions, along with two uni-directional vehicle underpasses at Miyapur Junction measuring 633 metres and 825 metres respectively

title: Coaching centre firing case: Patna court stays arrest of ‘Khan Sir’
content: The court also rejected the bail plea of Raushan Anand, the rival coaching institute owner and teacher who was earlier arrested in the case

title: UK MPs Write To British Foreign Secretary Over PoK Crackdown, Blackout
content: Over 50 British Parliamentarians have urged Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper to intervene as a communications blackout and security crackdown grip Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 400 Points As Traders Assess Fragile Iran Ceasefire
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices eased marginally after Israel and Iran signalled a pause in hostilities.

title: Delhi Professor Offered Her Tenants Water, Then A Murderous Plot Played Out
content: The accused struck the teacher on her head with the pestle that he had in his backpack and then slit her wrists with a razor.

title: Why A US Trade Law Is Holding Up Deal With India
content: The first phase of the bilateral trade agreement between India and the US can be finalised after the conclusion of two unilateral Section 301 investigations.

title: Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: Churn Within Trinamool After Poll Loss, MP Says Rebels "Power Hungry"
content: West Bengal Politics LIVE Updates: Crisis within the party escalated dramatically with a full-blown rebellion in the legislature and Parliament wings.

content: According to police, the accused have been identified as Nizam, said to be the General Secretary of Mangaluru Youth Congress, and Jitesh

title: Income Tax Return 2025-26: All About Rules, Refunds And Deadlines
content: Income Tax Returns 2025-26: The new tax regime may be more beneficial for those with fewer deductions and a preference for simpler compliance.

title: Man Strangles Lover's Husband, Leaves Body Under Bike, Tells Her 'Program Ok'
content: The victim, Kiran, allegedly used to harass his wife and that is said to have been a contributing factor in the murder plan.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Ignored By Rahul, Haunted By Pilot: Inside Ashok Gehlot's Grand Meltdown
content: Going by Gehlot's outburst, it remains unclear who he is accusing of 'conspiring'. But the 75-year-old seems to be getting uneasy about his growing irrelevance in the party.

title: Charlie Kirk's Death Prompted Usha Vance To Try For 4th Child, Says JD Vance
content: According to the US leader, the conversation with Erika Kirk changed "something" for Usha Vance, and "not long after we buried my friend, she became pregnant with our fourth child, a boy."

title: War In The Middle East Is Flaring Again. How Each Side Sees The Stakes
content: Israel and the US remain locked in a standoff with Iran and Hezbollah to shape the future regional order, with each side convinced it is acting from a position of strength.

title: "Nation First": PM Modi Lists Government's Key Achievements Over 12 Years
content: PM Modi said the government has worked with the spirit of "Nation First" and focused on empowering youth, women and farmers.

title: What Americans Think About US Intervention In Conflicts Abroad
content: While many Iranians were unhappy with their government, that did not necessarily mean they welcomed American or Israeli military strikes.

content: Senior MP Kalyan Banerjee today called this an "act of betrayal" but gave out a message of relief that the rebels had now revealed their loyalties.

title: Adani Energy Signs Pact To Buy Smart Meter Firm IntelliSmart For Rs 3,050 Crore
content: AESL said the transaction includes the acquisition of the entire equity share capital of IntelliSmart as well as the redemption of optionally convertible debentures held by NIIF.

title: No VIP Perks For Accused In Twisha Sharma Case, Say Jail Officials
content: The denial comes amid claims that the former judge and her lawyer son were being given special facilities in prison after being sent to 14-day judicial custody in the CBI-investigated case.

title: Three reports, one story: MiraOne to usher in new era of precision medicine through single blood test; grand launch in Mumbai
content: a comprehensive blood test that promises to transform the way we study our body was launched on Saturday in Mumbai at ImagiNXT conference attended by an impressive audience . MiraOne generates three reports to tell one story from a single draw of blood . gives a complete picture - your blood markers, the DNA you inherited and how you respond to medicines .
